Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Oct-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Harry Terzian, Esq., QUILL & ARROW, LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiffs.

Jennifer Stewart, Esq., ERSKINE LAW GROUP, PC, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

RE: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Not argued/contested. The court vacates its tentative ruling. Off-calendar. Motion was heard in the Discovery dept. on 10/9/23.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

PLAINTIFF GERMAN GARCIA-CANIZAL, BRIAN GARCIA NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE (TRANSFERRED TO DISCOVERY. MISCALENDARED IN THE LAW AND MOTION DEPT. BY THE MOVING PARTY)
Off Calendar Oct-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Joseph Saei, Esq. 213-443-4355 King & Spalding for Defendant.
No othe Appearance.***

THE PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTY AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON HIS BEHALF.

RULING - Mr. Saei informs the Court he does not contest the tentative ruling and submits to the authority of the Judge Pro Tem. Counsel discusses a "protective order". The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GERMAN GARCIA-CANIZAL, BRIAN GARCIA's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE.

Plaintiff German Garcia-Canizal's (Hereinafter Either "Plaintiff" Or "Garcia-Canizal") Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant General Motors, Llc (Hereafter Either "Defendant" Or "Gm") To Plaintiff's Requests For Production Of Documents (Rfp) Nos. 1 - 64 Is Granted In Part And Denied In Part As Follows:
(1) Defendant General Motors, Llc Boilerplate Objections To Rfp Nos 1- 64 Are Overruled Except As May Be Provided In Paragraph (2) Below.
(2) Within Twenty (20) Calendar Daysfrom The Date Of This Order, Defendant Will Deliver To Plaintiff, Without Objection, Full And Complete Veriified Code Compliant Responses To Rfp Nos 1-24, 26 - 29, 31- 40. 43 - 45, 47, 49 - 54, 60 & 61, As Well As Copies Of All Responsive Documents. To The Extent That Defendant Is Withholding The Production Of Any Responsive Document On The Basis Of Attorney/Client Privilege It Willdescribe The Document With Sufficient Specificity To Enable Plaintiff To Challenge Such Claim.
(3) Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Is Denied As To Rfp Nos 25, 30, 41, 42, 46, 48, 55 - 59 & 62 - 64.
(4) The Court Will Impose No Monetary Sanctions Against Any Party To This Litigation.

Mr. Saei to prepare a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and adds language re: protective order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Counsel to submit proposed order approved as to form directly to the Judge Pro Tem at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

***Mr. Donald Mahnke, another Counsel on Zoom informs the Court he is from the same firm as Counsel for Plaintiff and the matter is set for 10/10/23 at 9:30am. He will inform Counsel for PLaintiff as to what transpired at the hearing this morning. ***

PLAINTIFF GERMAN GARCIA-CANIZAL, BRIAN GARCIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE (TRANSFERRED TO DISCOVERY. MISCALENDARED IN THE LAW AND MOTION DEPT. BY THE MOVING PARTY)(ADDED FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY PART 2 OF 2)
